ip_outcome_note: Species name printed on card face; PokemonTCG.io API (swsh3-23) confirms name=Combusken, nationalPokedexNumbers=[256], Stage 1, evolvesFrom=Torchic. Depicted bipedal orange and cream bird in kicking pose amid flames matches canonical Combusken appearance exactly.

trivia_bullets.text: Combusken is National Pokedex no. 256, a Fire/Fighting-type Stage 1 introduced in Generation III (Pokemon Ruby and Sapphire, 2002). It evolves from Torchic at level 16 — the point at which the Fighting secondary type emerges — and evolves into Blaziken at level 36. [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh3-23; Bulbapedia: Combusken, via WebSearch snippet]

Combusken began a three-generation run of Fire/Fighting starter middle evolutions. Torchic (Fire-only) gains Fighting at first evolution; the same pattern repeated with Chimchar (Sinnoh) and Tepig (Unova), making Fire/Fighting the dominant Fire-starter secondary for Generations III through V. The streak was broken by Fennekin (Kalos, Generation VI), whose middle stage Braixen gained Psychic instead — a conscious design-level departure that was publicly noted by the community. [Bulbapedia / PokemonDB: Combusken / Blaziken / Emboar, via WebSearch snippet]
Flavor text: "During a battle, the hot flame in its body increases. Its kicks have outstanding destructive power." — printed on DAA-023. [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh3-23]

Art by kodama (a Pokemon TCG illustrator who signs works with the single lowercase name). The card places Combusken in a mid-air kick pose surrounded by swirling flame — a direct visual encoding of the flavor text's "kicks have outstanding destructive power" framing and the Fighting-type emergence at this evolutionary stage. [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh3-23 artist field]
